methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $200M - $3.5B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

data.narrative:
LanzaTech, a publicly traded Climate & Energy company founded by Sean Simpson and Richard Forster, carries a current valuation of $800M+ following $500M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 12 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables spanning both public and private markets, including high-profile private companies such as QuantumScape, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

The peer-median triangulation method applies directly to this analysis. Across the 12-company comp set, the median valuation stands at $850M, with a range spanning $200M at the low end to $3.5B at the high end. Notable comparables within this set include Form Energy at $3.5B (Series G), Sila Nanotechnologies at $3.3B (Late Stage), KoBold Metals at $2.0B (Series C), and Watershed at $1.8B (Series A). This wide dispersion reflects material differences in funding stage, technology maturity, and commercial progress across the Climate & Energy frontier-tech landscape.

Triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $722M to $977M, with a median point estimate of $850M. The current $800M+ valuation sits slightly below the peer median, suggesting modest relative discount positioning. Key uncertainties remain around commercial pathway execution, unit economics at scale, and regulatory trajectory—dimensions not fully captured in current-stage peer comparables.
